own_review.issue: Static firstDeploymentClassLoader is set once and never cleared anywhere (verified via grep at PR head: only declaration, null-check-assign, and use at line 708). In dev/continuous testing it pins the first deployment classloader and, via the new QuarkusClassLoader.curatedApplication back-reference, the entire first CuratedApplication for the JVM lifetime; later runs also load FacadeClassLoader through this possibly stale/closed classloader.

Local 'Collection<Runnable> shutdownTasks = new HashSet()' is passed to createAugmentor/makeCuratedApplication which adds curatedApplication::close to it, but the collection is never executed, returned, or stored - curated applications created on this path (every profile-keyed app made by FacadeClassLoader.getOrCreateCuratedApplication) are never closed: classloader/memory leak.

getTestClassesLocation(Class) for the 'quarkus' protocol delegates to getTestClassesLocation(Class, CuratedApplication), whose final fallback calls getTestClassesLocation(Class) again. If the class is not found in workspace module test sources or source classifiers (the maven custom test-source-set case the author's own TODO cites), this is infinite mutual recursion ending in StackOverflowError.

Catch of Exception|ServiceConfigurationError throws new IllegalStateException without chaining the caught exception as cause; the original failure is only emitted at debug level, destroying diagnostics for the very failure mode (#46048) the message tries to explain.

Static curatedApplications map: entries are never removed and never closed (close() only clears runtimeClassLoaders); the constructor unconditionally puts the no-profile key, clobbering a prior instance's entry. Combined with the discarded shutdownTasks in AppMakerHelper, every profile-specific CuratedApplication leaks. Human reviewer's point also lives here: the pre-discovery classloading design assumes extensions may add JUnit annotations via bytecode instrumentation, which deserves challenge.

loadClass throws RuntimeException instead of ClassNotFoundException on several failure paths (ClassLoader contract violation; JUnit handles CNFE gracefully but not RuntimeException), and leaves System.err.println debugging in production code.

The CurrentTestApplication consumer registration was removed from prepare(), so CurrentTestApplication.curatedApplication is never set in continuous testing; QuarkusMainTestExtension still checks it first and will now always bootstrap a fresh curated application in dev mode for @QuarkusMainTest (slow, duplicated app, the class's own new TODO admits it is 'only needed for QuarkusMainTest').
hackPendingDeferredDevServiceStart=true constant guard permanently disables H2 TCP server stop - acknowledged deliberate port leak shipped as dead-code if(false) branch.

configCleanup instance field is set in createAugmentor only when profile != null and run in getStartupAction's finally, but never nulled after running; a later getStartupAction call on the same AppMakerHelper instance (FacadeClassLoader holds one shared instance) with profile==null re-runs the stale cleanup, restoring system properties captured for a previous profile at the wrong time.
Comparator.comparing(o -> o.getTestClass().getClassLoader().getName()) NPEs if any test class is loaded by a classloader whose getName() returns null (plain URLClassLoader instances return null).
Parent-last behaviour only implemented for loadClass(String); getResource/getResources stay URLClassLoader-default and never consult fallbackParent, so class and resource visibility are inconsistent.

The captured 'resolver' delegate field is never used (dead state); each construction registers a fresh config and the instance is installed as the global ConfigProviderResolver per runtime classloader creation, dropping previously registered configs. Relatedly QuarkusTestExtension.ExtensionState.close() calls ConfigProviderResolver.setInstance(null), a JVM-global reset.
origCl is overwritten on every intercept invocation; close() restores the most recently captured TCCL rather than the original one, which can leave the wrong classloader installed after the session.

points.point: H2DevServicesProcessor hardcoded hackPendingDeferredDevServiceStart=true disables shutdown, port leak
points.note: Verified: constant-true guard makes tcpServer.stop() dead code; deliberate but real regression.

points.point: AbstractJvmQuarkusTestExtension.getCuratedApplication unchecked cast to QuarkusClassLoader, no error handling vs getClassLoaderFromTestClass
points.note: Verified at PR head lines ~98-104; bare cast with no diagnostics, inconsistent with the descriptive handling in QuarkusTestExtension.
Verified: gradle/maven workspace resolution block is duplicated nearly verbatim in both places; author TODO admits it.

points.point: TestResourceManager raw cast (Class<? extends QuarkusTestResourceLifecycleManager>) entry.clazz 'could fail at runtime'
points.note: Concern direction is reasonable but technically off: due to erasure the unchecked cast cannot fail at the cast site; any failure surfaces later. Not a verified defect as stated.

points.point: QuarkusTestExtensionState.clone may use serialization; ensure threads/Closeables handled
points.note: Hedged speculation; actual implementation (beyond v5's inline cut) is reflective getter copying, not serialization. No verified issue stated.

points.point: doJavaStart TODO about interceptor doing work upfront
points.note: Restates author's TODO, no issue identified.

points.point: StartupActionImpl TODO 'recreations in JUnitTestRunner for dev mode'
ClassCoercingTestProfile.testResources unsafe per its own TODO
points.note: Echoes the author's in-code TODO; no new analysis.

points.point: QuarkusClassLoader curatedApplication/startupAction back-references may prevent GC
points.note: Verified: curatedApplication is final, never nulled in close(); genuine retention concern matching the PR's admitted memory-footprint increase.

missed: Human's FacadeClassLoader design point: pre-discovery classloading justified by hypothetical bytecode-instrumented JUnit annotations - should be challenged (FacadeClassLoader.java, beyond v5's inline cut)
JunitTestRunner static firstDeploymentClassLoader never cleared - classloader/CuratedApplication leak and stale-loader use in continuous testing
AppMakerHelper.getStartupAction discards local shutdownTasks containing curatedApplication::close - profile curated applications never closed
PathTestHelper.getTestClassesLocation 'quarkus' protocol <-> CuratedApplication overload fallback mutual recursion -> StackOverflowError
AbstractJvmQuarkusTestExtension.evaluateExecutionCondition throws IllegalStateException without chaining the caught cause (debug-only logging)
exceeds: getCuratedApplication bare cast / missing error handling - real robustness gap not in the human review or my pre-grading review

points.point: Static firstDeploymentClassLoader never cleared; GC root chain via new QuarkusClassLoader.curatedApplication field; TestSupport/RuntimeUpdatesProcessor close() do not clear it
points.note: Verified by grep at PR head: field only ever assigned, never nulled; absence claims about close() methods are true. The flagship defect of the PR, with correct mechanism.

points.point: QuarkusClassLoader.curatedApplication final, never nulled in close(); per-profile runtime classloaders extend CuratedApplication lifetime
points.note: Verified: field is final, close() untouched; accurate retention analysis.

points.point: Fallback NPE: when FacadeClassLoader cannot be loaded, tests load via deployment CL which never has startupAction set; doJavaStart's getClassLoaderFromTestClass(...).getStartupAction() returns null -> NPE
points.note: Verified chain in code: StartupActionImpl only sets startupAction on runtime classloaders; doJavaStart dereferences unconditionally. Trigger window is narrow (e.g. InvocationTargetException in FCL construction) but the unguarded null path is real.

points.point: H2 explicit port-leak workaround, functional regression
QuarkusTestExtensionState.clone throws UnsupportedOperationException for any other subclass
points.note: Verified: clone() handles only the two hardcoded class names; IntegrationTestExtensionState (and user subclasses) exist and would fail if routed through the cross-classloader path.

points.point: Static runningQuarkusApplication moved to parent class without addressing per-classloader copies of statics
points.note: Verified: protected static in AbstractJvmQuarkusTestExtension; the PR's own getState() cross-CL clone machinery proves extension code runs under multiple classloaders, so per-CL static copies and stale references are a real hazard.

points.point: ConfigLauncherSession catch(ClassCastException) printStackTrace + IllegalStateException without cause
points.note: Verified: cause not chained, diagnosis message speculative; legitimate diagnostics issue.

points.point: @TestProfile on @Nested breaking change should go in release notes
points.note: Already listed by the author in the PR description under release-notes input.

points.point: junit.platform.launcher.interceptors.enabled=true is global to the JVM's JUnit platform

missed: Human's FacadeClassLoader design point: pre-discovery classloading justified by hypothetical bytecode-instrumented JUnit annotations - should be challenged
AppMakerHelper.getStartupAction discards local shutdownTasks containing curatedApplication::close - profile curated applications never closed
PathTestHelper.getTestClassesLocation 'quarkus' protocol <-> CuratedApplication overload fallback mutual recursion -> StackOverflowError
AbstractJvmQuarkusTestExtension.evaluateExecutionCondition throws IllegalStateException without chaining the caught cause
exceeds: Fallback NPE chain (deployment CL has no startupAction; doJavaStart dereferences unconditionally) - cross-file analysis neither the human nor my own review produced
clone() UnsupportedOperationException for state subclasses (IntegrationTestExtensionState) - verified gap not in the human review or my own review

qualitative: v6 is clearly stronger: it nails the headline defect (the never-cleared static firstDeploymentClassLoader) with an accurate GC-chain mechanism that exploits the full per-file diffs, correctly analyzes the QuarkusClassLoader back-reference retention, and contributes two genuine cross-file findings (the null-startupAction NPE fallback path and the clone() subclass gap) that neither the human nor my own first pass surfaced. v5 lands three real but second-tier points (H2 leak, bare cast, duplication) and pads the rest with TODO echoes and hedged speculation about code it could not see, missing every major lifecycle bug. Both candidates missed the human's FacadeClassLoader design challenge, the discarded shutdownTasks leak, the PathTestHelper mutual recursion, and the cause-swallowing in evaluateExecutionCondition. Neither candidate fabricated absences - v6's 'never cleared' claims all verified true against the PR head.
